VF_10_101.reported_concept: Shift in product mix away from high-value seasonal items, reducing average order value and thereby gross and net revenue

VF_10_211.reported_concept: Abnormal surge in discount spend (total discounts +60.1%) that amplified an underlying gross-revenue decline, together driving a 6.28% net-revenue drop.

VF_10_317.reported_concept: Sharp decline in seasonal product revenue causing a mix shift to lower-value orders

VF_10_431.reported_concept: Aggressive discount campaign expansion dramatically increasing total discounts applied

VF_10_547.reported_concept: Significant reduction in active discount campaigns

VF_15_101.reported_concept: Product-mix shift away from high-value seasonal items

VF_15_211.reported_concept: Expanded discount campaign activity driving excess discount deductions

VF_15_317.reported_concept: Decline in seasonal product sales (product-mix shift)

VF_15_431.reported_concept: Massive surge in discount spending driving down net revenue

VF_15_547.reported_concept: Sharp reduction in active discount campaign days

VF_35_317.reported_concept: Significant reduction in discount amounts applied to orders

VF_35_431.reported_concept: Massive surge in discount spend (340% increase) fully explains the net revenue decline despite higher gross revenue

VF_35_547.reported_concept: Significant reduction in discount spend driving net revenue uplift

VF_40_101.reported_concept: Surge in VIP customer order share

VF_40_211.reported_concept: Surge in discount spend (nearly doubling) that significantly eroded gross-to-net revenue conversion

VF_40_431.reported_concept: Aggressive expansion of discount campaigns suppressing net revenue growth

VF_40_547.reported_concept: Sharp reduction in discount spend (active discount campaigns)

DF_05_101.reported_concept: Massive reduction in discount activity (near-elimination of discounts applied to orders)

DF_05_211.reported_concept: Sharp reduction in active discount campaign coverage

DF_05_317.reported_concept: Complete cessation of discount campaigns in round R2

DF_05_431.reported_concept: Positive revenue surprise driven by a dual mix shift: increased VIP customer share and reduced seasonal-product mix, which together lifted average order value (AOV), compounded by modest order volume growth.

DF_05_547.reported_concept: Near-elimination of discount campaigns between rounds

DF_10_101.reported_concept: Near-elimination of discount campaigns between rounds

DF_10_211.reported_concept: Dramatic reduction in discount activity between rounds

DF_10_317.reported_concept: Dramatic reduction in discount campaign activity, which simultaneously depressed order volume and gross revenue while also sharply reducing discount spend — leaving net revenue only modestly lower.

DF_10_431.reported_concept: Surge in discount amounts applied to orders

DF_10_547.reported_concept: Massive reduction in discount spend (−90.78%) between rounds

DF_40_317.reported_concept: Reduced discount campaign activity leading to lower order volume

DF_40_431.reported_concept: Massive surge in discount spend erasing gross revenue gains

DF_40_547.reported_concept: Excessive discount expansion eroding net revenue growth

DF_45_101.reported_concept: Reduction in discount spend offsetting a decline in gross revenue, producing a near-zero net change in post-discount revenue (m_101).

DF_45_211.reported_concept: Massive surge in discount spend erasing gross revenue gains

DF_45_317.reported_concept: Expanded discount campaign activity driving a disproportionate surge in total discounts, offsetting gross revenue growth and causing net revenue to decline.

DF_45_431.reported_concept: Massive surge in applied discounts driving net revenue down despite gross revenue growth

DF_45_547.reported_concept: Increased discount campaign frequency driving disproportionate discount spend

SF_05_101.reported_concept: Mass de-flagging (or loss) of seasonal product tags between rounds, causing nearly all items previously classified as 'seasonal' to lose that designation.

SF_05_211.reported_concept: Collapse in seasonal product mix (share of seasonal items ordered)

SF_05_317.reported_concept: Mass removal of the 'seasonal' product flag between rounds

SF_05_431.reported_concept: Collapse in the share of seasonal products ordered (m_111 dropped from 33.1% to 4.1%), which directly caused the ~87% decline in seasonal revenue (m_107).

SF_05_547.reported_concept: Collapse in seasonal product availability or flagging

SF_10_101.reported_concept: Collapse in the share of seasonal products ordered (m_111 −69.5%), which directly caused the proportional fall in seasonal revenue (m_107 −68.9%)
